AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED 4 MILES NORTHWEST OF RIO VISTA AND TRAVELED EAST APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES. A GARAGE WAS DAMAGED AND A HOUSE WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION NEAR THE BEGINNING OF THE TRACK. NEAR THE END OF THE TRACK, 5 HOMES WERE DAMAGED WITH ONE SUSTAINING HEAVY DAMAGE. THIS TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS WIDE WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100-110 MPH.
TREE DOWN OFF BEACH STREET NORTH OF I-820 IN NORTH FORT WORTH

AN EF1 TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED IN ALVARADO BY A NWS STORM SURVEY. A MOBILE HOME WAS OVERTURNED RESULTING IN TWO MINOR INJURIES. THE EQUINE VET BUILDING WAS DAMAGED AND FOUR OTHER COMMERCIAL BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. THIS TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 200 YARDS LONG, 150 FEET WIDE, WITH ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 90 MPH.
STRAIGHT LINE WINDS RESULTED IN THE COLLAPSE OF A WALL OF THE MEDICINE CHEST PHARMACY NEAR NORTH CUMMINGS DR AND HIGHWAY 67. SIGN AND POWER POLE DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED IN THE SAME AREA.

AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE NORTHERN PORTIONS OF FORNEY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF FM 740 AND LOVERS LANE. FIVE HOMES SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. THIS TORNADO WAS 60 YARDS WIDE, A HALF MILE LONG, WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100-105 MPH.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO BEGAN IN THE TOWN OF CASH AND TRACKED SEVEN MILES TO THE NORTHEAST. A TOTAL OF 6 MOBILE HOMES, A MANUFACTURING PLANT, AND TWO METAL BUILDINGS SUSTAINED DAMAGE. ONE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. THE DAMAGE WAS NON-CONTINUOUS ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO WHICH WAS 200 YARDS WIDE. ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 100-110 MPH.

A NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED AN EF-0 TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 1 MILE NORTHWEST OF HONEY GROVE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 2781 AND HIGHWAY 182. THE TORNADO TRAVELED 1.4 MILES WITH A MAX DAMAGE WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 2875 AND PRIVATE ROAD 287.THE MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 85 MPH. SEVERAL STRUCTURES SUFFERED DAMAGE AND TREES WERE ALSO DAMAGED.
